untranslatable_reason: Broadening Formation requires identification of two distinct trend lines (top and bottom) with diverging slopes, which cannot be expressed with available point-in-time indicators. The pattern depends on multi-bar trend line construction and slope measurement across multiple bars, requiring pattern recognition that transcends single-bar indicator conditions. The exit measure rule (height calculation between highest high and lowest low, then projection) is also geometric/structural rather than indicator-based.
